title: Morning Triage Extended
instruction: Process your morning inbox. Star the two emails that mention an end-of-day deadline or need your sign-off. Archive the newsletter in your Promotions tab and the FYI update in your Primary tab. Forward the email that asks you to loop in a colleague — find the correct person from the email body. Reply to the email from Maya Morris about the project update (Reply only, not Reply All). Reply to the email from Nina Wright confirming receipt. Then create an 'Action Required' label and apply it to the two starred emails.
eval_check_descriptions: Action Required label applied to both starred

emails.subject: Contract review before close of business
emails.body:
I need this reviewed before end of day. The vendor is expecting our sign-back by tomorrow morning.

Please prioritize this over the other items on your plate today.

Thanks,
Maya

emails.subject: Urgent: Updated project timeline
emails.body:
FYI only — no action needed from you. The project timeline was updated yesterday and I wanted you to have the latest version.

Everything is on track and the team is handling the remaining items.

Thanks,
Jordan

emails.subject: Procurement approval — needs your sign-off
emails.body:
The procurement order is ready to go but needs your sign-off before we proceed.

Finance will not release the PO without your confirmation on file.

Thanks,
Theo

emails.subject: Weekly industry digest — top stories this week
emails.body:
This week's top stories in operations and supply chain management.

Unsubscribe at any time by clicking the link at the bottom of this email.

Thanks,
Summit Metrics

emails.subject: Facilities update — no action needed
emails.body:
Quick update: the office HVAC maintenance has been completed ahead of schedule.

No action needed on your end. Just keeping you in the loop.

Thanks,
Avery

emails.subject: Integration spec needs engineering input
emails.body:
Could you please loop in Avery Kim from engineering? They worked on the original integration spec and would know the current constraints.

I think we can finalize the API contract once they weigh in on the rate limits.

Thanks,
Elena

emails.subject: Northstar vendor recovery plan — project update
emails.body:
Here is the latest project update. We are on track for the milestone next week.

Let me know if any of the deliverables need to be reprioritized.

Thanks,
Maya

emails.subject: Shipment confirmation needed
emails.body:
Could you confirm receipt of the Q1 shipment manifest?

The warehouse team needs your acknowledgment before they release the next batch.

Thanks,
Nina

emails.subject: Updated rollout checklist
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

Most of the edits were minor, with one budget line still waiting on approval. Happy to consolidate comments if more feedback comes in.

Thanks,
Jordan

emails.subject: Customer feedback from pilot cohort
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

Most of the edits were minor, with one budget line still waiting on approval. If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner summary before the meeting.

Thanks,
Jordan

emails.subject: Follow-up on venue estimate
emails.body:
Sending a quick status update from today's working session.

Most of the edits were minor, with one budget line still waiting on approval. Happy to consolidate comments if more feedback comes in.

Thanks,
Nina

emails.subject: Updated rollout checklist
emails.body:

Most of the edits were minor, with one budget line still waiting on approval. If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner summary before the meeting.

Thanks,
Priya

Most of the edits were minor, with one budget line still waiting on approval. Happy to consolidate comments if more feedback comes in.

Thanks,
Marcus

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. Happy to consolidate comments if more feedback comes in.

Thanks,
Priya

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Thanks,
Miles
